an3.54:1.1: Then a certain brahmin wanderer approached the *Bhagavā*; having approached … he sat down to one side. Seated to one side, that brahmin wanderer said this to the *Bhagavā*:
an3.54:1.2: "'The *dhamma* is visible here and now, the *dhamma* is visible here and now,' it is said, Master Gotama.
an3.54:1.3: In what way, Master Gotama, is the *dhamma* visible here and now, immediate, inviting one to come and see, leading onwards, to be known by the wise each for themselves?"
an3.54:2.1: "When one is inflamed by passion, brahmin, overcome by passion, their mind [*citta*] consumed, they intend harm to themselves, they intend harm to others, they intend harm to both; and they experience mental *dukkha* and dejection.
an3.54:2.2: When passion is abandoned, they no longer intend harm to themselves, no longer intend harm to others, no longer intend harm to both; and they no longer experience mental *dukkha* and dejection.
an3.54:3.1: When one is inflamed by passion, brahmin, overcome by passion, their mind [*citta*] consumed, they conduct themselves badly in body, badly in speech, badly in mind.
an3.54:3.2: When passion is abandoned, they no longer conduct themselves badly in body, no longer badly in speech, no longer badly in mind.
an3.54:4.1: When one is inflamed by passion, brahmin, overcome by passion, their mind [*citta*] consumed, they do not know their own good as it truly is, they do not know another's good as it truly is, they do not know the good of both as it truly is.
an3.54:4.2: When passion is abandoned, they know their own good as it truly is, they know another's good as it truly is, they know the good of both as it truly is.
an3.54:4.3: In this way too, brahmin, the *dhamma* is visible here and now …
an3.54:5.1: When one is corrupted by hatred, brahmin …
an3.54:5.2: when one is deluded by delusion, brahmin, overcome by delusion, their mind [*citta*] consumed, they intend harm to themselves, they intend harm to others, they intend harm to both; and they experience mental *dukkha* and dejection.
an3.54:5.3: When delusion is abandoned, they no longer intend harm to themselves, no longer intend harm to others, no longer intend harm to both; and they no longer experience mental *dukkha* and dejection.
an3.54:6.1: When one is deluded by delusion, brahmin, overcome by delusion, their mind [*citta*] consumed, they conduct themselves badly in body, badly in speech, badly in mind.
an3.54:6.2: When delusion is abandoned, they no longer conduct themselves badly in body, no longer badly in speech, no longer badly in mind.
an3.54:7.1: When one is deluded by delusion, brahmin, overcome by delusion, their mind [*citta*] consumed, they do not know their own good as it truly is, they do not know another's good as it truly is, they do not know the good of both as it truly is.
an3.54:7.2: When delusion is abandoned, they know their own good as it truly is, they know another's good as it truly is, they know the good of both as it truly is.
an3.54:7.3: In this way, brahmin, the *dhamma* is visible here and now, immediate, inviting one to come and see, leading onwards, to be known by the wise each for themselves."
an3.54:8.2: May Master Gotama remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge from this day forth, for as long as life lasts."
